title: TRIED Eval Spec
description: Fully-determined spec for one eval-set candidate. Produced by the stage-1 sampler before any LLM call. Every semantically load-bearing decision (op set, shapes, dtypes, tolerance, seed) is fixed here so the LLM in stage 2 only fills in code. AST guardrails in stage 3 verify the produced pytorch_code matches this spec; /preflight in stage 4 verifies the function actually runs and agrees eager-vs-Inductor under the spec's tolerance_policy.

spec_id.description: Deterministic UUIDv5 derived from (form, ops, input_shapes, input_dtypes, rng_seed, form_metadata). Stable across re-runs of the sampler so accepted/rejected status is joinable across iterations.

tier.description: Difficulty tier. Determines target n in the eval split (105 / 115 / 80) and downstream stratified reporting. Top-level (not derived from form) so stratification is direct without joining against the forms registry. Same closed vocabulary as the existing Difficulty enum.

form.description: Closed vocabulary of fusion shapes. Tier-restricted: each form belongs to exactly one tier, enforced by the stage-1 sampler. The form determines which prompt template stage 2 renders, which output-shape function stage 1 calls, and which tolerance-policy function fills the tolerance_policy field.

ops.description: Ordered list of base ops to compose, drawn from BASE_OPS in packages/shared/src/shared/eval/base_ops.py. Stage 3 AST validation rejects pytorch_code that calls anything outside this allow-list. Length must satisfy the form's op-count rule (Easy=2, Medium=3, Hard>=4). Names are canonical (e.g. 'torch.nn.functional.gelu', 'torch.relu', 'torch.add').

input_shapes.description: Shape per input tensor. Length matches input_dtypes. Sampled by stage 1 from the form's shape grid.

input_dtypes.description: Dtype per input tensor. Length matches input_shapes.

expected_output_shape.description: Output shape derived deterministically from (form, ops, input_shapes, form_metadata) by stage 1. Passed to the LLM as a sanity check and verified post-execution in stage 4.

expected_output_dtype.description: Output dtype derived deterministically from (form, ops, input_dtypes) by stage 1. Most forms preserve dtype; argmax flips to int64; mixed-dtype forms follow PyTorch promotion rules.

tolerance_policy.description: Derived deterministically from (form, expected_output_dtype) by stage 1 — never picked by the LLM. Same closed vocabulary as DatasetRow.attempts.correctness.tolerance_policy_used; the implementation lives in packages/shared/src/shared/verification/tolerance.py.

rng_seed.description: RNG seed for input fixture generation. Fixed per spec for reproducibility — re-running /preflight on the same spec must produce the same eager and Inductor outputs.

form_metadata.description: Form-specific knobs. Each form documents its own required keys in forms.py; the stage-2 prompt renderer reads them, and per-form make_inputs(spec, rng) helpers read them when building the input fixtures. Examples: {'reduce_dim': -1} for unary_then_reduction; {'num_heads': 8, 'causal': false, 'scale': 'auto'} for attention_qkv; {'num_embeddings': 4096, 'embedding_dim': 256, 'norm_eps': 1e-5} for embedding_then_norm. May be {} for forms that need no extra knobs (e.g. chain_2_unary). additionalProperties is true here ONLY — the top-level schema stays tight.
